select_when: A system has many moving parts, but only a few intervention points are likely to change behavior materially.
Resources are limited, so the team must decide where one action can alter flow, incentives, or constraints disproportionately.
Repeated effort at the surface has failed, and the real opportunity likely sits deeper in the system structure.
The work needs a disciplined way to prioritize what to do and what not to do instead of boiling the ocean.
avoid_when: Preferred initiatives are being labeled as leverage points without evidence that they sit near a real bottleneck, feedback loop, or governing rule.
The team is wading into analysis before clearly defining the system structure and the candidate intervention point.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on finding disproportionate intervention points and prioritizing scarce effort rather than on broad systems rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor topology or scoring overlays because the raw source is stronger on structural prioritization and anti-ocean-boiling discipline.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 scope.
open_questions: The typed output compresses a source that ranges from system redesign to communication focus and capital-allocation framing.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that leverage points are strongest when many moving parts exist but a few intervention points matter disproportionately.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by the source danger lines on fake leverage labeling and premature analysis.

input_type.note: The raw source activates on structural prioritization and system intervention problems; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source yields prioritized intervention focus and clearer levers; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Leverage points clearly require systems and diagnostic reasoning, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
